meaning: adj.人员不足的,船员不足的
examples: In some stores we were undermanned and customer service was suffering.
Unfortunately, it is underfunded, undermanned and lacks the authority to make rules that everyone must follow.
But undermanned and underarmed, Naw Kham was able to take control of areas surrounding the Triangle with remarkable ease.
Though undermanned, Border Patrol and Coast Guard officials try to disrupt the smuggling trips before they begin.
Sure, the Pac-12 game is a mismatch thanks to sanctions against USC that put an undermanned UCLA team in the game.
